sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 1 action in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: LEFT.
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 203, level 1.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, MOUSE, ACTION7.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 202 actions; a strong score needs about 61 or fewer. You are 3.3x over the target.
If you are not steadily making progress toward the level goal, commit to your single best hypothesis and the shortest sequence that tests it — do not exhaustively scan rows/columns or enumerate every option. Test one idea, read the result, then decide.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
OK, the game is no longer "game_over" after I took a LEFT action. The game state is "NOT_FINISHED" again. The blocks are still at the right wall. Let me check the current state.
Looking at the image:
- Red, Blue, Green are still at the right wall
- Agent is at the left, no path visible
- The game is NOT game_over anymore
So the game didn't reset, but the game_over flag was cleared. The blocks are still at the right wall in the wrong order. I need to figure out how to rearrange them.
Wait, but the agent has no path to the blocks. How can I interact with them?
Let me check the segmentation to understand the current state better.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current state - game over cleared but blocks still at wall
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
print("Current nodes:")
for n in nodes:
    if n['pixels'] >= 8 and n['color'] not in ['B', 'c', 'g', 'G']:
        b = n['boundary']
        row_center = sum([p[0] for p in b]) / len(b)
        col_center = sum([p[1] for p in b]) / len(b)
        print(f"  color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, row~{row_center:.1f}, col~{col_center:.1f}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The blocks are still at the right wall. The agent is at row 38.5, col 13.5. The game is not over anymore, but the blocks are stuck at the right wall.
Looking at the image, the agent has no path to the blocks. I need to figure out how to reset the blocks to the left side.
Wait, maybe I need to move the agent to connect to a block? Let me try moving RIGHT to see if a path appears.
Actually, looking at the image more carefully, there's no path from the agent to the blocks. The agent is at the left side, and the blocks are at the right wall. The agent needs to connect to a block to interact with it.
Let me try moving RIGHT to see if the agent can connect to a block.
But wait, the agent is at row 38.5, and the blocks are at rows 20.5, 26.5, 32.5. So the agent is below all the blocks. Moving RIGHT won't connect to any block.
Let me try moving UP to get to a block's row, then RIGHT to connect.
Actually, I think the game might have a "reset" mechanic. Let me try moving the agent to the starting position or some special location.
Wait, looking at the image, the agent is at the left side with no path. Maybe I need to move to one of the block rows to reconnect?
Let me try moving UP to row 32.5 (Green's row), then RIGHT to connect to Green.
Actually, the agent is at row 38.5. The blocks are at 20.5, 26.5, 32.5. So I need to move UP 6 rows to reach 32.5.
But wait, the vertical track is at col 13.5 (the left side). The agent is at col 13.5. So I can move UP/DOWN along the track.
Let me try moving UP to 32.5, then RIGHT to connect to Green.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Agent at 38.5, blocks at 20.5, 26.5, 32.5 (all at right wall)
# Try moving UP to 32.5 (Green's row) then RIGHT to reconnect
action(['UP'] * 6)
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
